{"step_by_step_thinking": "The tetrazolium chloride staining is used to assess myocardial viability. In the early stages of myocardial infarction, the necrotic area appears pale, while the uninfarcted area appears brick red. Since the patient died on the way to the hospital, the myocardium would still show the characteristic brick red color due to the presence of viable tissue. Therefore, the color of the myocardium would be red.", "answer_choice": "A"}
